title: Create global groups
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to create global groups that are available across the entire platform, so that I can define standardized groupings that apply to all tenants for policy, eligibility, and security purposes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am authenticated as the HRMS/Platform Administrator, when I create a group and mark its scope as global, then the group is created with global scope and becomes available platform-wide across all tenants; Given I am creating a global group, when I omit a required name or reuse an existing global group name, then the system rejects the creation with a validation error; Given a global group exists, when a non-Platform-Administrator (Portfolio Admin, Group Company Admin, Company Admin) opens group settings, then they can view but cannot create, edit, or delete global groups; Given global groups exist, when any tenant views available groups, then the global groups are visible for use as policy, eligibility, and security criteria.
source: FR 6.6.1 (global groups created by HRMS Administrator)

title: Create company-specific groups
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create groups scoped to my own company, so that I can group my company's employees and users independently of other tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Company/HR Admin, when I create a group, then the group is scoped to my company only and is not visible or usable by other companies; Given a company-specific group is created, when it is referenced elsewhere in the platform (policies, eligibility, security), then it is available only within its owning company's context; Given I attempt to create a group, when I do not have permission for the target company scope, then the system prevents creation; Given both global and company-specific groups exist, when I view my company's group list, then I see global groups (read-only) plus my company's own groups.
source: FR 6.6.1 (company-specific groups)

title: Create groups shared across companies in a group company
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to create groups that span the companies within my group company, so that I can define groupings shared across the member companies I administer.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a Group Company Admin, when I create a group at group-company scope, then the group is available to the companies belonging to my group company and is not visible to companies outside it; Given a group-company-scoped group exists, when a Company Admin of a member company configures policies or eligibility, then that group is selectable within the group company's context; Given I am a Group Company Admin, when I attempt to create a global group, then the system prevents it because global scope is reserved for the Platform Admin.
source: FR 6.6.1 (global vs company-specific groups; group-company scope)

title: Build hierarchical parent-child group structures
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to arrange groups into parent-child hierarchies, so that I can model organizational and policy groupings that reflect nested relationships.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am managing groups, when I assign a parent to a group, then the group is nested under that parent as a child and the relationship is persisted; Given a group hierarchy exists, when I view a group, then its parent and child relationships are visible; Given I attempt to set a parent, when the chosen parent would create a cycle (e.g., selecting a descendant as parent), then the system rejects the assignment; Given a parent group is selected, when I add a child, then the child must be within a compatible scope (a company group cannot parent a global group).
source: FR 6.6.2 (hierarchical parent-child structures)

title: Support n-level group nesting
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want group hierarchies to support unlimited (n-level) nesting, so that I can represent deep organizational structures without depth restrictions.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group hierarchy exists, when I add child groups beyond a single level, then the system supports nesting at any number of levels; Given a deeply nested structure, when I create a new child at any depth, then the system does not reject it due to a nesting-level limit; Given a multi-level hierarchy, when I view or navigate the tree, then the full n-level parent-to-descendant path is displayed correctly.
source: FR 6.6.2 (n-level nesting)

title: Edit and delete groups and reparent hierarchies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to rename, move, and delete groups, so that I can maintain the group structure as the organization changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group exists, when I rename or change its parent, then the change is saved and reflected in the hierarchy and in any references; Given a group has child groups, when I attempt to delete it, then the system requires me to reassign or remove the children (or blocks deletion) so that no orphaned children remain; Given a group is used as policy applicability criteria, when I attempt to delete it, then the system warns about or prevents deletion to avoid breaking active policies; Given I edit or delete a group, when the group is not within my authorized scope, then the action is denied.
source: FR 6.6.1, FR 6.6.2, FR 6.6.4 (group lifecycle within scope)

title: Assign employees to multiple groups
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign an employee to more than one group, so that a single employee can be covered by multiple groupings simultaneously.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record exists, when I add the employee to multiple groups, then the employee is recorded as a member of each of those groups; Given an employee belongs to several groups, when I view the employee's group memberships, then all groups they belong to are listed; Given an employee is already a member of a group, when I add them again to the same group, then the system prevents duplicate membership; Given an employee is a member of groups, when I remove them from one group, then their membership in the other groups is unaffected.
source: FR 6.6.3 (employees may belong to multiple groups)

title: Include non-user employees in groups
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want non-user employees (those without login accounts) to be assignable to groups, so that policy and eligibility groupings cover the entire workforce, not only employees who log in.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee has no user/login account (Employee Non-User), when I add them to a group, then their membership is recorded the same as for user employees; Given a non-user employee is a group member, when policies are evaluated for that group, then the non-user employee is included in the applicable population; Given a non-user employee, when they are a group member, then no login capability is granted by the membership itself.
source: FR 6.6.3 (employees may belong to multiple groups)

title: Associate users with groups
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to associate users with groups where required, so that group-based security and access can be applied to users, not only employees.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user account exists, when I associate the user with a group, then the user is recorded as a member of that group; Given a user is associated with a group, when I review the group's membership, then the associated user appears; Given a user, when association with a group is not required for a scenario, then the system does not force a user-to-group association; Given a user is associated with a group used for security, when access is evaluated, then the group membership contributes to the user's security context.
source: FR 6.6.3 (users may be associated with groups where required)

title: View my own group memberships
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view the groups I belong to, so that I can understand which groupings and policies apply to me.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authenticated Employee (User) who is a member of one or more groups, when I open my profile or memberships view, then all groups I belong to are listed; Given I am not a group administrator, when I view my memberships, then I can see but cannot modify group membership or group definitions; Given group memberships change, when I next view my memberships, then the current, updated list is shown.
source: FR 6.6.3 (employees may belong to multiple groups)

title: Use groups as policy applicability criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to select groups as applicability criteria when defining policies, so that policies apply to the members of the chosen groups.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group exists, when I configure a policy's applicability, then I can choose that group as an applicability criterion; Given a policy is scoped to a group, when the policy is evaluated, then it applies to all members (employees and associated users) of that group; Given a group is hierarchical, when a policy targets a parent group, then applicability resolves consistently across the parent and its members per the defined rule; Given global, group-company, and company groups exist, when I set policy applicability, then only groups within my authorized scope are selectable as criteria.
source: FR 6.6.4 (groups available as policy applicability criteria)

title: Govern group usage and scope across the portfolio
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want visibility and governance over how global and company groups are used across the companies in my portfolio, so that grouping remains consistent for policy, eligibility, and security.
acceptance_criteria: Given global and company-specific groups exist across my portfolio, when I review groups, then I can see the group scope (global, group-company, company) and where each is used; Given a group is used as policy applicability criteria, when I audit policy scoping, then I can trace which groups drive which policies; Given governance controls apply, when I attempt an action outside my portfolio's scope, then the system denies it.
source: FR 6.6.1, FR 6.6.4 (group scope and policy applicability governance)

title: Preserve effective-dated group membership history
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want group memberships to be effective-dated and their history retained, so that policies and eligibility can be evaluated correctly as of any point in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I add or remove an employee or user from a group, when the change is saved, then the membership is stored with effective-from/effective-to dates rather than overwriting prior state; Given a member left a group in the past, when I evaluate or report group membership as of an earlier date, then the historical membership is returned rather than the current one; Given a correction is made to a past membership, when I record it, then the prior value is retained as history (bitemporal) so both the corrected and the originally-recorded views are auditable; Given memberships change over time, when I query current membership, then only records effective as of today are returned.
source: FR 6.6.3 (L1 domain/data; effective-dated membership history)

title: Enforce tenant-scoped isolation for groups
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want group data to be tenant-scoped and isolated at the persistence layer, so that company-specific groups and memberships never leak across tenants while global groups remain readable everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: Given company-specific and group-company groups are stored, when any query or API reads groups, then row-level security restricts results to the requesting tenant's authorized scope; Given a global group exists, when a tenant reads groups, then the global group is readable across tenants but is not writable outside the Platform Admin; Given a uniqueness constraint on group name within a scope, when a duplicate name is inserted at the same scope, then the persistence layer rejects it; Given a cross-tenant reference is attempted (e.g., adding another company's employee to a company group), when it is persisted, then integrity constraints block the operation.
source: FR 6.6.1, FR 6.6.3 (L1 domain/data; tenant-scoped RLS and integrity)

title: Manage groups as governed versioned config consumed by engines
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want group definitions and hierarchies to be governed, versioned, effective-dated configuration that the shared engines read, so that grouping changes take effect without code and remain auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Given group definitions and parent-child hierarchy exist, when they are changed, then the change is captured as a new version with an effective date rather than silently mutating prior config; Given the rules and policy engines reference groups, when a group's definition or hierarchy version changes, then the engines pick up the governed config at runtime without any code deployment; Given a group config version is active, when I review governance, then I can see who changed it, when it takes effect, and which prior version it supersedes; Given a group is referenced by active policies, when I change its config, then the versioned reference remains resolvable so historical evaluations stay reproducible.
source: FR 6.6.4 (L2 config/metadata; groups as versioned governed config)

title: Resolve group-based applicability through the rules engine
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want the rules engine to correctly resolve my group memberships (including nested groups) when it evaluates policy applicability, so that the right policies apply to me automatically.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy targets a group, when the rules engine evaluates applicability, then it expands the group's membership to include employees and associated users that belong to it; Given the targeted group is a parent in the hierarchy, when the engine resolves applicability, then it traverses n-level descendants per the configured inheritance rule and includes members of child groups; Given an employee belongs to multiple groups, when overlapping policies are evaluated, then the engine returns the correct combined applicable set without double-counting the member; Given membership is effective-dated, when the engine evaluates as of a given date, then it uses the membership effective on that date.
source: FR 6.6.4 (L3 engine; rules/eligibility resolution of group membership)

title: Manage groups through a tree-based self-service screen
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven screen to navigate, search, and edit the group hierarchy and memberships, so that I can maintain groups efficiently without technical help.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group hierarchy exists, when I open the groups screen, then an n-level tree is rendered with expand/collapse and the current scope (global, group-company, company) indicated per node; Given many groups exist, when I search or filter by name or scope, then the tree filters to matching groups and their ancestors; Given I reparent a group in the UI (e.g., drag onto a new parent), when the move would create a cycle or cross an incompatible scope, then the UI blocks it and shows a clear error; Given I open a group, when I view its membership grid, then I can add or remove employees and users in bulk and see counts update, with actions limited to my authorized scope.
source: FR 6.6.2 (L4 presentation; group hierarchy and membership management UI)

title: Maintain statutory benefit-eligibility cohort groups
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to maintain groups that represent statutory benefit-eligibility cohorts (such as ESI, Gratuity, Group Health Insurance, and Physically Challenged), so that employees eligible for each statutory scheme are grouped for benefit administration and payroll processing.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Group screen, when I add a new group and give it a benefit-cohort name such as ESI or Gratuity, then the group is saved as a benefit-eligibility cohort and listed; Given a benefit cohort exists (e.g., Gratuity), when the associated statutory policy is evaluated, then only members of that cohort are treated as eligible for the benefit; Given multiple statutory cohorts exist, when I view the group list, then each benefit cohort is shown as a distinct group; Given a cohort group is referenced by a benefit or payroll policy, when I attempt to delete it, then the system warns or blocks deletion to protect the active benefit configuration.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (ESI/Gratuity/Group Health Insurance/Physically Challenged benefit cohorts)

title: Capture a rich description and statutory eligibility notes per group
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to record a descriptive name and a free-text description/eligibility note on each group, so that anyone reviewing the group understands its purpose and the statutory basis (e.g., Payment of Gratuity Act 1972) for membership.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am adding or editing a group, when I enter a Name and a Description, then both are saved and the Description can hold detailed eligibility criteria or statutory references; Given groups exist, when I view the group list, then each row shows the group Name and its Description; Given a description references a statutory act or benefit rule, when a reviewer opens the group, then the full note is legible and preserved verbatim; Given I omit the Name, when I try to save, then the system rejects the save with a validation error while the Description remains optional.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Name and Description fields with statutory eligibility notes)

title: Edit a group inline from the group list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want an Edit action on each group row in the list, so that I can quickly update a group's name or description without leaving the list screen.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group is displayed in the list, when I click the Edit action on that row, then the group opens in an editable form pre-filled with its current Name and Description; Given I change the details and save, when I return to the list, then the row reflects the updated values; Given the group is outside my authorized scope, when I open the list, then the Edit action for that group is disabled or hidden; Given I cancel the edit, when I return to the list, then the group retains its original values.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (per-row Edit action)

title: Page through and refresh the group list with item counts
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the group list to be paginated with a visible item count and a Refresh control, so that I can review large numbers of groups and always see the current set.
acceptance_criteria: Given more groups exist than fit on one page, when I use the pager, then subsequent pages of groups load and the control shows a count such as "Displaying items 1 - N of M"; Given the group list is displayed, when I click Refresh, then the list reloads with the current groups and counts; Given groups were added or removed in another session, when I refresh, then the newly current set and updated total count are shown; Given I am on a later page, when the total shrinks below that page, then the pager falls back to a valid page rather than showing an empty view.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (pagination, item count, Refresh)

title: Search and filter the group list by name
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to search or filter the group list by name, so that I can locate a specific group quickly among many benefit and organizational cohorts.
acceptance_criteria: Given many groups exist, when I type a search term, then the list filters to groups whose name matches the term; Given a filter is applied, when no group matches, then the list shows an empty-state message rather than stale rows; Given I clear the search, when the filter is removed, then the full group list and its total count are restored; Given search is combined with scope, when I search, then only groups within my authorized scope are returned in the results.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (name-based list search)

title: Activate or deactivate a group without deleting it
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to mark a group Active or Inactive, so that I can retire a cohort from new use while preserving its definition and historical membership.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group exists, when I set its status to Inactive, then the group is retained but is excluded from selection when configuring new policies or memberships; Given a group is Inactive, when I set it back to Active, then it becomes selectable again; Given a group is Inactive, when historical evaluations run as of a date when it was Active, then its prior membership and applicability still resolve correctly; Given a group is referenced by an active policy, when I deactivate it, then the system warns that dependent policies may be affected.
source: Kensium: Organization > Employee Classification (Active/Inactive status), applied to Group

title: Filter the group list by Active or Inactive status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to filter the group list by Active or Inactive status, so that I can focus on the cohorts currently in use or review retired ones.
acceptance_criteria: Given active and inactive groups exist, when I select Active and apply the filter, then only active groups are listed; Given I select Inactive and apply the filter, when the list reloads, then only inactive groups are shown; Given a status filter is active, when I view counts, then the displayed item count reflects only the filtered subset; Given I reset the filter, when the list reloads, then groups of all statuses are shown again.
source: Kensium: Organization > Employee Classification (status filter + Display), applied to Group

title: See member counts on each group in the list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each group row to show how many employees and users it contains, so that I can verify cohort sizes at a glance before applying policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given groups with members exist, when I view the group list, then each row shows the current count of members (employees and associated users); Given I add or remove a member, when the list refreshes, then the affected group's count updates accordingly; Given a group has no members, when I view it, then the count shows zero rather than a blank; Given membership is effective-dated, when the count is displayed, then it reflects members effective as of today.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (list membership visibility)

title: Auto-populate statutory cohort membership from eligibility criteria
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want membership of a statutory benefit cohort to be populated automatically from defined eligibility criteria, so that employees who qualify (e.g., under the Payment of Gratuity Act) are included without manual assignment.
acceptance_criteria: Given a benefit cohort has eligibility criteria configured, when an employee meets the criteria, then they are automatically added to the cohort group; Given an employee ceases to meet the criteria, when eligibility is re-evaluated, then they are removed from the cohort with the change effective-dated; Given auto-population runs, when an employee was manually added as an exception, then the manual membership is preserved and flagged distinctly from auto-derived membership; Given I review a cohort, when I open its membership, then I can see which members were derived by rule versus added manually.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Gratuity cohort per Payment of Gratuity Act 1972)

title: Set an applicability scope on a group
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set an applicability value on a group (for example limiting it to a location or work area), so that the group targets only the intended segment of the organization.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating or editing a group, when I set its applicability (such as a location, work area, or organization scope), then the applicability is saved with the group; Given a group has an applicability, when I view the group list, then the applicability is shown alongside the name; Given a group's applicability is set to a specific location, when policies use the group, then only members within that applicability are targeted; Given the applicability is outside my authorized scope, when I attempt to save, then the system prevents it.
source: Kensium: Organization > Work Area (Applicability), applied to Group

title: Group employees into payroll cohorts by class type frequency and wage type
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to group employees by employee-class attributes such as class type, payroll frequency, and wage type, so that payroll runs correctly for each cohort of employees.
acceptance_criteria: Given I define an employee-class-based group, when I set its class type, payroll frequency, and wage type, then employees of that classification are grouped for payroll processing; Given such a cohort exists, when payroll is processed, then the group's payroll frequency and wage type drive the calculation for its members; Given I view the cohort, when I open the list, then the class type, payroll frequency, and wage type are displayed; Given an employee's classification changes, when it is updated, then their membership in the corresponding payroll cohort updates effective-dated.
source: Kensium: Organization > Employee Classification (class type, payroll frequency, wage type), applied to Group

title: Clone an existing group to seed a new cohort
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to duplicate an existing group's definition (name pattern, description, applicability, and criteria) as the basis for a new group, so that I can create similar cohorts quickly without re-entering details.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group exists, when I choose to clone it, then a new editable group is pre-filled with the source group's description, applicability, and eligibility criteria but with a distinct name; Given I clone a group, when I save the copy, then it is created as an independent group without carrying over the source group's members unless I opt in; Given I attempt to save a clone with a name that duplicates an existing group in the same scope, then the system rejects it with a validation error; Given the source group is outside my authorized scope, when I attempt to clone it, then the action is denied.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Add new group workflow reused for cloning)

title: Export the group membership list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to export a group's membership (and the full group list) to a file, so that I can share cohort composition for audits, benefit enrollment, or reconciliation.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group with members exists, when I export its membership, then a file is produced listing each member with identifying details effective as of the export date; Given I export the full group list, when the export completes, then it includes each group's name, description, status, applicability, and member count; Given the data is tenant-scoped, when I export, then only groups and members within my authorized scope are included; Given an export is generated, when I review it, then effective-dated membership reflects the point-in-time chosen for the export.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (list export for audit/reconciliation)

title: Assign a short acronym or code to each group
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign a short acronym or code to each group in addition to its name, so that groups can be referenced compactly in payroll files, reports, and integrations.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am adding or editing a group, when I enter a short acronym alongside the group name, then the acronym is saved and displayed with the group; Given an acronym must be unique within scope, when I reuse an existing acronym in the same scope, then the system rejects the save with a validation error; Given a group has an acronym, when it is exported or used in payroll files, then the acronym identifies the cohort compactly; Given I leave the acronym blank, when I save, then the system either derives one or accepts the name alone per configuration.
source: Kensium: Organization > Employee Classification (Employee class acronym), applied to Group

title: View a read-only group detail
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a read-only View action on each group that shows its full definition and membership, so that I can inspect a cohort without risking accidental edits.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group is listed, when I click the View action on its row, then the group opens in a read-only detail showing its name, acronym, description, status, applicability, hierarchy position, and members; Given I am in the read-only view, when I attempt to change a field, then editing is disabled and I must use Edit to make changes; Given the group is outside my authorized scope, when I open View, then only permitted, non-sensitive attributes are shown; Given I close the View, when I return to the list, then no changes are persisted.
source: Kensium: Organization > Work Area (View action), applied to Group

title: Cancel and discard an unsaved group
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a Cancel action while adding or editing a group, so that I can abandon changes without creating or altering a group.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Add new group or edit form, when I click Cancel, then the form closes and no new group is created and no edits are persisted; Given I have entered partial data, when I Cancel, then the entered values are discarded and the list is shown unchanged; Given I edited an existing group and Cancel, when I reopen it, then it retains its original values; Given unsaved changes exist, when I Cancel, then the system may prompt to confirm discarding before leaving.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Cancel action)

title: Save the group step and continue the org setup sequence
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to save the Group configuration and proceed to the next organization setup step, so that I can complete the guided organization configuration in sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have configured groups on the Group screen, when I click Next or Save & Next, then the group configuration is saved and the next organization setup screen opens; Given required group data is missing or invalid, when I click Next, then the system blocks navigation and shows validation errors; Given I advance to the next step, when I return to the Group step, then my saved groups are preserved; Given the setup is part of a guided sequence, when I complete each step, then progress through the organization configuration is tracked.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Next) and Head Office (Save & Next stepped setup)

title: Navigate between related organization configuration screens
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to move between the Group screen and adjacent organization screens such as Work Area and Role from the same navigation, so that I can configure related organizational structures without losing context.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am on the Group screen, when I select the Work Area or Role tab from the navigation, then that screen opens while my saved group data is retained; Given I switch screens with unsaved changes, when I navigate away, then the system warns me to save or discard first; Given navigation tabs are shown, when I lack permission for an adjacent screen, then that tab is hidden or disabled; Given I return to the Group screen, when it reloads, then the current group list is displayed.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (Work Area / Role navigation tabs)

title: Designate a group as static or dynamic
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to designate whether a group is static (manually maintained) or dynamic (membership derived from rules), so that cohorts are maintained by the appropriate method.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am creating or editing a group, when I set its type to static, then membership is managed only by manual add and remove; Given I set the type to dynamic, when I define its membership rule, then the system evaluates the rule and populates members automatically; Given a group is dynamic, when I attempt to manually add a member outside the rule, then the system either blocks it or flags it as an exception; Given I switch a group from dynamic to static, when I confirm, then the current derived members are frozen as manual members.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (rule-derived vs manually maintained cohorts)

title: Configure the inheritance rule for nested groups
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to configure how membership and policy applicability are inherited across a parent-child group hierarchy, so that nested groups resolve consistently for the engines.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group hierarchy exists, when I set the inheritance rule to inherit-downward, then members of a parent group are included in the applicable evaluation for its descendants per the rule; Given I set the rule to no-inheritance, when policy applicability resolves, then only the directly targeted group's members are included; Given an inheritance rule is set at a parent, when a child overrides it, then the most specific rule governs that child's resolution; Given the rule changes, when the rules engine next evaluates, then it applies the updated inheritance without code changes.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (hierarchical parent-child membership resolution)

title: Bulk import group memberships from a file
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to import group memberships from a spreadsheet, so that I can onboard large cohorts quickly instead of adding members one at a time.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have a file of employee identifiers for a group, when I import it, then matched employees are added as members and the results are summarized; Given some rows fail to match or are outside my scope, when the import completes, then those rows are reported as errors without blocking valid rows; Given the import adds members, when it is saved, then each new membership is effective-dated as of the import date; Given a member in the file is already in the group, when imported, then the system skips the duplicate rather than erroring.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (counterpart to membership export/import)

title: Transfer employees between groups in bulk
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to move a selected set of employees from one group to another in a single action, so that I can reorganize cohorts efficiently when the structure changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given employees belong to a source group, when I select them and transfer to a target group, then they are removed from the source and added to the target with effective-dated changes; Given a selected employee is already in the target group, when the transfer runs, then their target membership is not duplicated; Given the target group is outside my authorized scope, when I attempt the transfer, then the action is denied; Given the transfer completes, when I view both groups, then the source and target member counts update accordingly.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (bulk membership management)

title: Merge two groups into one
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to merge one group into another, so that I can consolidate redundant cohorts while preserving membership.
acceptance_criteria: Given two groups exist, when I merge the source into the target, then all source members are added to the target without duplication and the source is retired; Given the source group is referenced by active policies, when I merge, then the system warns me and requires re-pointing or confirmation before retiring the source; Given the merge runs, when it completes, then historical membership of the source is preserved for point-in-time evaluation; Given either group is outside my authorized scope, when I attempt the merge, then the action is denied.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (group lifecycle and consolidation)

title: Assign an owner or steward to a group
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to assign a responsible owner or steward to each group, so that accountability for a cohort's membership and eligibility is clear.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group exists, when I assign an owner, then the owner is recorded and shown with the group; Given a group has an owner, when membership or eligibility changes are proposed, then the owner is identified as the accountable party; Given the owner leaves or changes, when I reassign ownership, then the change is saved with history; Given I view the group list, when owners are set, then each group's owner is visible within my authorized scope.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (governance/ownership of cohorts)

title: Approve group membership change requests
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want membership additions and removals for sensitive groups to require my approval, so that cohort changes affecting benefits or security are controlled.
acceptance_criteria: Given a group is marked to require approval, when a Company Admin requests a membership change, then the change is held in a pending state until an approver acts; Given a pending request exists, when I approve it, then the membership change is applied and effective-dated as of approval; Given I reject the request, when it is rejected, then no membership change is applied and the requester is notified with the reason; Given a group does not require approval, when a change is made, then it applies immediately without a pending state.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (approver-controlled membership for benefit/security cohorts)

title: Be notified when my group membership changes
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to be notified when I am added to or removed from a group, so that I understand changes to the policies and benefits that apply to me.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am added to a group, when the change takes effect, then I receive a notification indicating the group and effective date; Given I am removed from a group, when the change takes effect, then I am notified of the removal; Given a membership change is corrected retroactively, when it is recorded, then the notification reflects the corrected state; Given I am a member of a policy-only group, when its membership changes, then notifications follow the configured rules for that group.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (membership change visibility to members)

title: Review the audit trail of group changes
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to review a chronological audit trail of changes to group definitions, hierarchy, and membership, so that I can demonstrate compliance and investigate discrepancies.
acceptance_criteria: Given groups and memberships change over time, when I open a group's audit trail, then I see who made each change, what changed, and when it took effect; Given a group's parent or applicability was changed, when I review the trail, then both the prior and new values are shown; Given membership was added or removed, when I audit, then each membership event is listed with its effective date and actor; Given I attempt to view audit for a group outside my portfolio scope, when I open it, then access is denied.
source: Kensium: Organization > Group (L2 governance; change audit of versioned group config)
